title: Ophthalmologists, Except Pediatric
description: Diagnose and perform surgery to treat and help prevent disorders and diseases of the eye. May also provide vision services for treatment including glasses and contacts.

tasks.text: Perform comprehensive examinations of the visual system to determine the nature or extent of ocular disorders.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Diagnose or treat injuries, disorders, or diseases of the eye and eye structures including the cornea, sclera, conjunctiva, or eyelids.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide or direct the provision of postoperative care.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or implement plans and procedures for ophthalmologic services.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prescribe or administer topical or systemic medications to treat ophthalmic conditions and to manage pain.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop treatment plans based on patients' histories and goals, the nature and severity of disorders, and treatment risks and benefits.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform ophthalmic surgeries such as cataract, glaucoma, refractive, corneal, vitro-retinal, eye muscle, or oculoplastic surgeries.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Educate patients about maintenance and promotion of healthy vision.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Document or evaluate patients' medical histories.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform, order, or interpret the results of diagnostic or clinical tests.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide ophthalmic consultation to other medical professionals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Refer patients for more specialized treatments when conditions exceed the experience, expertise, or scope of practice of practitioner.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform laser surgeries to alter, remove, reshape, or replace ocular tissue.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams of health professionals to provide optimal patient care.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prescribe corrective lenses such as glasses or contact lenses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prescribe ophthalmologic treatments or therapies such as chemotherapy, cryotherapy, or low vision therapy.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Instruct interns, residents, or others in ophthalmologic procedures and techniques.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct clinical or laboratory-based research in ophthalmology.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
